subject: Public Health
title: Intellectual Property and Access
description: One-minute skill: Intellectual Property and Access.

hook.text: Patents save innovation—but during pandemics, they can block the poorest countries from making vaccines.

buildup.text: Vaccine patents give companies exclusive manufacturing rights for 20 years—protecting their investment.

discovery.text: During COVID-19, calls for a TRIPS waiver sought to suspend patents and enable generic vaccine production.

twist.text: Even without patents, manufacturing know-how and technology transfer are the real barriers to production.

climax.text: The mRNA Technology Transfer Hub in South Africa is sharing knowledge—building long-term manufacturing capacity.

punchline.text: A patent waiver without technology transfer is an empty gesture.

quiz.question: What is the TRIPS waiver in the context of vaccines?
options: A proposal to suspend patents to enable generic vaccine production
A trade agreement about vaccine imports
A quality standard for vaccine manufacturing
